ORDER AUTHORIZING FILING OF PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD, AND SETTING FEBRUARY 4, 2019 HEARINGS

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT INDICATES IT WAS INFORMED YESTERDAY THAT RESPONDENT HAD SUBMITTED A PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD FOR E-FILING WHICH WAS SUBSEQUENTLY REJECTED BY E-FILING FOR LACK OF PAYMENT OF LOCAL FEES, LACK OF A RESERVATION NUMBER, AND LACK OF AN INDICATED HEARING DATE AND TIME. PETITIONER HAD SUBMITTED AN OPPOSITION TO THAT PETITION TO VACATE. THE COURT INDICATES IT WILL ISSUE A FORMAL ORDER AUTHORIZING RESPONDENT TO FILE HIS PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD. RESPONDENT MUST RESUBMIT HIS PETITION FOR E-FILING. PETITIONER STANDS ON THE OPPOSITION ALREADY SUBMITTED. THE COURT PERMITS RESPONDENT TO FILE A REPLY BRIEF TO HIS PETITION TO VACATE BY JANUARY 28, 2019. A HEARING ON THE PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION IS SET FOR FEBRUARY 4, 2019 AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPT. 302, LAW & MOTION. AS TO PETITIONER'S PETITION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD, THE COURT CONTINUES TODAY'S HEARING ON THAT MATTER TO FEBRUARY 4, 2019 AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPT. 302, LAW & MOTION. THE COURT DOES NOT PERMIT FURTHER BRIEFING ON THIS MATTER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: SEAN KANE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600, MARIA.TORREANOM@GMAIL.COM (302/HK)

PETITIONERS NOELLE BONNER AND THE NOBO, LLC'S PETITION TO CONFIRM THE AMENDED FINAL ARBITRATION AWARD DATED NOVEMBER 15, 2018 IS GRANTED, AND RESPONDENTS ALEX KWAK AND APPLEPALM ENTERPRISES, INC.'S PETITION TO VACATE THAT AWARD IS DENIED. THE COURT WILL ENTER JUDGMENT CONFIRMING THE AWARD IN FAVOR OF THE NOBO, LLC AND AGAINST RESPONDENT APPLEPALM ENTERPRISES, INC. calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing And Petition To Correct Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Jun-05-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jay Song, Esq. 213-935-0099 of Logos Professional Law Corporation for Respondent ALEX KWAK, APPLEPALM ENTERPRISES, INC. through Court Call.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Discussed. The Court did not receive an email contesting the tenatative ruling. Respondent informs the Court he was not aware of the tentative ruling. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

RESPONDENT ALEX KWAK, APPLEPALM ENTERPRISES, INC.'s Petition To Correct Arbitration Award.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Respondent Alex Kwak and Applepalm Enterprises, Inc.'s petition to correct arbitration award is treated as a motion to correct a clerical mistake in the court's judgment, and is denied as to the request to correct the amount of the award and is granted as to the incorrect naming of Alex Kwak as a judgment debtor.

The court has the inherent power under Code of Civil Procedure Section 473(d) to correct clerical mistakes in its judgment. The motion to correct the arbitration award as to the sum awarded is both untimely and unfounded. Because the court intended to confirm the arbitration award, to extent that the judgment failed to conform to the arbitration award, the court retains inherent power to correct the judgment nunc pro tunc and excise from the judgment "Alex Kwak" as a person against whom recovery is adjudged. (See Ames v. Paley (2001) 89 Cal.App.4th 668, 674 ["because the trial court intended to enter judgment pursuant to the settlement agreement, to the extent the judgment failed to conform to the terms of the settlement agreement, the trial court retained the inherent power to correct the judgment nunc pro tunc some three months after entry of the judgment."].)(as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Hearing
Off Calendar Feb-04-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Bruce Methven, Esq., Telephone 510-649-4019, for Plaintiff; Appearing by Telephone call from the Court;
Jay Song, Esq., Logos Professional Law Corp., Telephone 877-529-1004 for Defendant;

This motion was contested late. The Court received an email from defendant's counsel contesting this matter on Sunday February 3, 2019 at 7:34 p.m. Plaintiffs counsel was called by the court clerk so to that he may appear in this matter. Plaintiff's counsel states on the phone that he revieced the contest email on Sunday at 7:34 p.m. but did not actually view the email until this morning February4, 2019. Court admonishes Defendnats counsel for not following proper procedures regardin contesting a calendared motion and not providing sufficeint notice to all parties.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER NOELLE BONNER's Notice Of Hearing.

Petitioners Noelle Bonner and The Nobo, LLC's petition to confirm the amended final arbitration award dated November 15, 2018 is granted, and Respondents Alex Kwak and Applepalm Enterprises, Inc.'s petition to vacate that award is denied. The Court will enter judgment confirming the award in favor of The Nobo, LLC and against Respondent Applepalm Enterprises, Inc. per Code of Civil Procedure section 1287.4.

The scope of judicial review of arbitration awards is extremely narrow. An arbitrator's decision generally is not reviewable for errors of fact or law. (Moncharsh v. Heily & Blase (1992) 3 Cal.4th 1, 11; Branches Neighborhood Corp. v. Calatlantic Group, Inc. (2018) 26 Cal.App.5th 743, 750.) Respondents have not shown either that the arbitrator exceeded his powers or that their rights were substantially prejudiced by the arbitrator's refusal to postpone the hearing upon sufficient cause being shown therefor or by his refusal to hear evidence material to the controversy. (Code Civ. Proc. 1286.2(a)(4),(5).) The arbitrator's award of damages bore a rational relationship to the contract and the breach found by the arbitrator. (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. v. Intel Corp. (1994) 9 Cal.4th 362, 381; San Francisco Housing Authority v. Service Employees Internat. Union, Local 790 (2010) 182 Cal.App.4th 933, 943-944.) Respondents have not shown that the arbitrator refused to continue the hearing after the date for the hearing was set. Nor have Respondents offered any admissible evidence to contradict the arbitrator's express finding that he considered the evidence offered at the hearing, which included Respondents' proffered evidence.

Order was submitted in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Hearing
Off Calendar Jan-15-2019 Continued To Feb-04-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Petitioner NOELLE BONNER (Tel: 650-753-2831) appearing in pro per;
Atty. JAY SONG, ESQ. of Logos Professional Law Corp. (Tel: 888-529-1004) appearing for Respondent;
ADAM TRUEBLOOD (Tel: 510-846-2176) also present as Petitioner's partner.

The Court indicates it was informed yesterday that Respondent had submitted a petition to vacate arbitration award for e-filing which was subsequently rejected by e-filing for lack of payment of local fees, lack of a reservation number, and lack of an indicated hearing date and time. Petitioner had submitted an opposition to that petition to vacate. The Court indicates it will issue a formal order authorizing Respondent to file his petition to vacate arbitration award. Respondent must resubmit his petition for e-filing. Petitioner stands on the opposition already submitted. The Court permits Respondent to file a reply brief to his petition to vacate by January 28, 2019. A hearing on the petition to vacate arbitration is set for February 4, 2019 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302, Law & Motion.

As to Petitioner's petition to confirm arbitration award, the Court continues today's hearing on that matter to February 4, 2019 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302, Law & Motion. The Court does not permit further briefing on this matter.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Sean Kane, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600, maria.torreanom@gmail.com (302/HK)
